creator: lawrance@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 20/11/2006, at 9:49 AM, Pav Lucistnik wrote:

> Synopsis: Update graphics/osg to match dependent packages
>
> State-Changed-From-To: open->feedback
> State-Changed-By: pav
> State-Changed-When: Sun Nov 19 18:01:45 UTC 2006
> State-Changed-Why:
> Does not install:
>
> ===>  Checking if graphics/osg already installed
> gmake[1]: Entering directory `/work/a/ports/graphics/osg/work/ 
> OSG_OP_OT-1.0/OpenSceneGraph'
> gmake[2]: Entering directory `/work/a/ports/graphics/osg/work/ 
> OSG_OP_OT-1.0/OpenSceneGraph'
> Syntax error: word unexpected (expecting "done")
> gmake[2]: *** [__install] Error 2
> gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/work/a/ports/graphics/osg/work/ 
> OSG_OP_OT-1.0/OpenSceneGraph'
> gmake[1]: *** [__instbin] Error 2
> gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/work/a/ports/graphics/osg/work/ 
> OSG_OP_OT-1.0/OpenSceneGraph'
> gmake: *** [install] Error 2
> *** Error code 2
>
> Stop in /a/ports/graphics/osg.
> ================================================================

Odds on that the makefile needs spaces added to the end of those  
shell lines, much like the previous update did.
